title: Census 2027 and Aadhaar
focus: Covers the framework for the 2027 digital census and the features and statutory basis of Aadhaar.
rationale: Fallback section derived from packet-level grouping.
sectionExcerpt:
[Page 058]
8. GOVERNANCE AND SOCIAL ISSUES

8.1. CENSUS 2027

Why in the News?
Cabinet approves scheme of Conduct of Census of India 2027.

Key Details of Census 2027
- Phased Conduct: The exercise will occur in two phases: (i) Houselisting and Housing Census (April to September 2026) and (ii) Population Enumeration (PE) (February 2027).
- First Digital Census: Using mobile applications.
- Caste Census Data: Census will capture Caste data during the Population Enumeration phase.
- New Technology: A dedicated Census Management & Monitoring System (CMMS) portal has been developed for real-time monitoring of the process.
- Census-as-a-service (CaaS) will deliver data to ministries in a clean, machine-readable and actionable format.
- Participation: The public will be provided with an option to self-enumerate.

INDIA'S CENSUS: A NATIONAL SNAPSHOT
THE FRAMEWORK
- A Constitutional Mandate: The Census is a Union subject under Article 246 of the Constitution.
- Governed by The Census Act, 1948: This act provides the legal foundation for conducting the entire census process.
- Conducted by the Registrar General's Office: This body operates under the Ministry of Home Affairs.
HISTORY & SCOPE
- Gathers Micro-Level Data: Information includes demography, housing, religion, and literacy down to the village level.
- 2021 Census Postponed: The decennial census was delayed due to the Covid-19 pandemic.
- First Census Held in 1872: The most recent census was completed in 2011.

Registrar General and Census Commissioner
- Appointed under the Registration of Births and Deaths Act, 1969.
- Ministry: Ministry of Home Affairs.
- Key functions:
 - Housing & Population Census
 - Civil Registration System (CRS): Under the Registration of Births & Deaths (RBD) Act (1969) which provides for the compulsory registration of births and deaths.
 - Others: Sample Registration System, National Population Register, Mother Tongue Survey, etc.

[Page 059]
8.2. AADHAAR
Why in news?
Union Ministry of Agriculture has made Aadhaar authentication mandatory for individuals and farmer groups to avail benefits under e-NAM (National Agriculture Market) scheme.
Features of Aadhaar
- Uniqueness: 12-digit unique identification number and no resident can have a duplicate number since it is linked to their individual biometrics. It is issued by UIDAI and can also be deactivated or omitted by UIDAI.
- Random Number: It does not capture details like caste, religion, income, heal...
